concepts.label: On-device speech recognition
concepts.def: Automatic speech-to-text transcription run locally through platform-native APIs (e.g. Apple's SpeechAnalyzer) instead of cloud services, benchmarked against models like Whisper.
concepts.ev: Latest AI/tech trends (2026-07-13 HN): 'On-device speech AI benchmarking' theme + NOTABLE 'Apple SpeechAnalyzer API' compared against Whisper, signaling a shift to local inference.

concepts.label: Second-Life Hardware
concepts.def: Repurposing decommissioned, consumer-grade, or e-waste accelerators for AI inference to lower cost and amortize already-embodied manufacturing carbon over a longer service life.
concepts.ev: Latest-trends 'Retro hardware & constraint hacking' theme — benchmarking e-waste GPUs and 'doing more with old, constrained hardware' (HN front page, 2026-07-13).

concepts.label: Permacomputing
concepts.def: A design philosophy that maximizes the useful lifespan of computing hardware and minimizes energy and material waste by doing more with old, constrained machines rather than replacing them.
concepts.ev: HN trend theme 'Retro hardware & constraint hacking: Booting Linux on ancient consoles and benchmarking e-waste GPUs reflects a strong culture of doing more with old, constrained hardware' (updated 20

concepts.label: Linus's Law (limits of)
concepts.def: The claim that 'given enough eyeballs, all bugs are shallow' — empirically undermined when a serious defect survives many years of open peer review, showing visibility alone does not guarantee discovery.
concepts.ev: HN trend theme 'Systemic security & surveillance rollback': a 15-year-old kernel bug topped the front page, signalling renewed scrutiny of long-lived risk in widely-reviewed open-source code.

concepts.label: Hardware memory tagging
concepts.def: A CPU mechanism (e.g. Arm MTE) that tags allocations and pointers so the hardware traps use-after-free and out-of-bounds accesses at runtime, catching the class of foundational memory-safety bugs that mitigations like the borrow checker address only in safe languages.
concepts.ev: HN front-page theme: a 15-year-old use-after-free surfacing across every distro shows foundational memory-safety bugs remain top-of-mind; hardware memory tagging is an established, durable mitigation

concepts.label: Digital preservation
concepts.def: The practice of durably archiving and redundantly mirroring public datasets and content so they survive deletion, link rot, or censorship.
concepts.ev: HN front-page trend theme: 'Open-data preservation: Stories about rescuing destroyed public datasets show a community valuing durable, mirrorable open information.'

concepts.label: Infinite canvas
concepts.def: An unbounded, pannable and zoomable 2D workspace for spatially arranging, linking, and exploring notes, nodes, and media.
concepts.ev: HN trend: 'Local-first knowledge tools: Graph/canvas note apps are resurging as people want to own and connect their own data' plus 'Explorable spatial/3D visualizations' Show HN hits.

concepts.label: Bidirectional linking
concepts.def: A knowledge-graph primitive where a link between two notes is navigable from both ends, surfacing backlinks that reveal the connective structure of a corpus.
concepts.ev: HN trend: 'Local-first knowledge tools: Graph/canvas note apps are resurging as people want to own and connect their own data.'

concepts.label: Level-of-detail rendering
concepts.def: A rendering technique that substitutes coarser geometry or fewer primitives as objects recede or the view zooms out, keeping large real-data 3D scenes interactive in the browser.
concepts.ev: trend theme 'Explorable spatial/3D visualizations: Multiple huge Show HN hits are navigable maps and worlds... zoomable, real-data spaces in the browser'

concepts.label: LOCKSS (many-replica preservation)
concepts.def: Lots Of Copies Keep Stuff Safe — a preservation paradigm that keeps public data durable by mirroring it across many independent, self-auditing replicas so no single takedown or failure can destroy it.
concepts.ev: Trend theme 'Open-data preservation: Stories about rescuing destroyed public datasets show a community valuing durable, mirrorable open information.'

concepts.label: Exceptional access (mandated backdoor)
concepts.def: A government-mandated interception or decryption pathway built into communications infrastructure, which weakens security for everyone by creating a privileged access path that adversaries can also discover and abuse.
concepts.ev: Trend theme 'Surveillance & security erosion: the front page is dominated by ... surveillance-infrastructure stories, signalling anxiety about who watches and what breaks silently.'

concepts.label: Patch gap (n-day window)
concepts.def: The exploitable window between a vulnerability's public patch and its deployment across systems, during which attackers reverse-engineer the fix to build n-day exploits against the still-unpatched.
concepts.ev: Trend theme 'Surveillance & security erosion: the front page is dominated by long-lived vulnerabilities ... that break silently.'

concepts.label: Machine Unlearning
concepts.def: Techniques for removing the influence of specific training data from an already-trained model without full retraining, in response to privacy, takedown, or consent demands.
concepts.ev: Latest AI/tech trends theme: 'Surveillance & AI-training data backlash — Institutions and users are actively rejecting mass surveillance and forced data harvesting for AI.'

concepts.label: Training-data consent
concepts.def: Norms and machine-readable signals (do-not-train opt-outs, consent registries, provenance-based exclusion) that let people and institutions withhold their content from AI training corpora.
concepts.ev: Latest trends theme 'Surveillance & AI-training data backlash': institutions and users are actively rejecting mass surveillance and forced data harvesting for AI.

concepts.label: Consent-or-Pay
concepts.def: A data-collection model in which a service conditions core access on the user either consenting to have their data used for AI training or paying a fee, rendering that consent effectively coerced.
concepts.ev: Latest trends theme 'Coerced data for AI training: Companies tying core service access to consent for AI training is triggering user backlash.'

concepts.label: Surveillance capitalism
concepts.def: The economic model in which firms extract behavioral and personal data as raw material for prediction and AI training, driving the mass-surveillance and coerced-consent dynamics now provoking public and institutional backlash.
concepts.ev: HN front-page trend theme (2026-07-13): 'Surveillance & data-privacy backlash: public and institutional pushback against mass surveillance and coerced AI-training data is dominating the front page.'

concepts.label: Coding ligatures
concepts.def: Font ligatures in monospace code faces that fuse character sequences (e.g. != into a single glyph), which can be disabled so literal source characters render exactly as typed.

concepts.ev: HN trend theme 'Open-data resilience & preservation: When institutional data is taken down, open mirrors and archives are what keep it alive.'

concepts.label: Rust rewrite (RIIR)
concepts.def: The recurring practice of reimplementing foundational systems software in idiomatic Rust to gain memory safety and correctness guarantees the original language could not enforce.
concepts.ev: Latest AI/tech trends theme: 'Rust rewrites of foundational systems: Reimplementing classic systems in idiomatic Rust remains a top signal of the language's pull for correctness and safety.'

concepts.label: Data commons

concepts.label: Streaming ASR

concepts.label: Word Error Rate (WER)

concepts.label: Speaker Diarization

concepts.ev: Latest-trends theme 'On-device speech & audio AI' — local, benchmarked audio/speech models (Apple SpeechAnalyzer vs Whisper); diarization is a core companion task in that on-device speech stack.

concepts.label: Procedural Generation

concepts.label: Takeoff economics

concepts.label: Graph database
concepts.def: A database whose native data model is nodes and edges with properties (a property/object graph), optimized for traversing richly connected data rather than joining flat relational tables.
concepts.ev: Latest-trends theme 'Graph databases and knowledge modeling: Object-oriented graph stores are resurging as a way to model richly connected data' (HN front page, 2026-07-14)

concepts.label: Object database
concepts.def: A datastore that persists interconnected in-memory objects by identity with transparent navigation between them, rather than decomposing them into relational rows or explicit edge tables.
concepts.ev: latest AI/tech trends theme: 'Graph databases and knowledge modeling — Object-oriented graph stores are resurging as a way to model richly connected data.'

concepts.label: TinyML / embedded inference
concepts.def: The practice of running machine-learning models on microcontrollers and other resource-constrained embedded substrates rather than on servers or cloud GPUs.
concepts.ev: Latest AI/tech trends theme 'On-device / embedded ML: Momentum is toward running models locally and in unexpected substrates rather than always calling the cloud.'

concepts.label: Graph query language
concepts.def: A declarative language for matching and traversing patterns over a property graph (e.g. the ISO GQL standard and Cypher), serving as the graph analogue of SQL.
concepts.ev: Latest-trends theme (HN front page 2026-07-14) on graph databases as the backbone for structured knowledge; complements the existing SQL-serving cluster (typed-sql, text-to-sql, query-planner).

concepts.label: Property graph model
concepts.def: A graph data model in which both nodes and edges carry typed labels and arbitrary key/value properties, distinct from the storage engine itself, and the shape trending object-oriented graph databases and this system's ontology store both adopt.
concepts.ev: Trend theme (2026-07-14 HN front page): object-oriented graph databases trending as the backbone for structured knowledge, mirroring the site's knowledge graph; the system stores its ontology as a lab

concepts.label: Triplestore (RDF)
concepts.def: A database that stores knowledge as subject-predicate-object triples under the RDF model and queries them with SPARQL, a graph paradigm distinct from the labeled-property-graph model.
concepts.ev: Trend theme 'Object-oriented graph databases are trending as the backbone for structured knowledge' plus the system's own brain/ontology.json knowledge graph maintained by ontology_learn.py/ontology_m

concepts.label: Cloud Development Environment
concepts.def: A remote, fully-provisioned, standardized coding workspace (devcontainers, Codespaces, throwaway VMs) that replaces the developer's local machine as the place where code and autonomous agents run, then is discarded after use.
concepts.ev: HN front-page trend theme 'Sandboxed coding agents' + Clawk ('gives coding agents a throwaway Linux VM so they never touch your real machine') + trend theme 'Building without heavy IDEs/toolchains'

concepts.label: Index-Free Adjacency
concepts.def: The native graph-database storage technique where each node holds direct physical references to its neighbours, so traversals follow pointers in constant time instead of computing repeated index lookups or joins.
concepts.ev: latest AI/tech trend theme 'Graph / object databases: General-purpose graph databases for connected data are drawing front-page attention' — index-free adjacency is the defining storage technique that

concepts.label: Multi-model database
concepts.def: A database that stores and queries data under several data models (graph, document, object, key-value) through a single engine and query layer.
concepts.ev: Hacker News NOTABLE (2026-07-14): YouTrackDB, an object-oriented graph database for general-purpose connected-data workloads — an engine spanning object+graph models

concepts.label: LLM jury
concepts.def: Aggregating verdicts from a panel of several diverse LLM evaluators instead of a single judge, to label data and score outputs more reliably and at lower cost.
concepts.ev: latest AI/tech trends theme 'LLMs as evaluators and juries': 'Using panels of LLMs to judge and label data is emerging as a practical building pattern.'

concepts.label: Weak supervision
concepts.def: Training or evaluating models on large volumes of cheap, noisy labels — increasingly produced by panels of LLMs labeling data — instead of costly hand-annotation.
concepts.ev: Latest-trends theme 'LLMs as evaluators and juries': using panels of LLMs to judge and label data is emerging as a practical building pattern.

concepts.label: Knowledge-graph embedding
concepts.def: Encoding a knowledge graph's entities and relations as dense vectors so connected concepts can be retrieved and reasoned over by similarity rather than exact traversal.
concepts.ev: 2026-07-14 HN trend theme 'Graph databases & knowledge graphs' (YouTrackDB object-oriented graph DB) intersected with this system's own JSON knowledge-graph ontology (brain/ontology.json).

concepts.label: LLM-as-annotator
concepts.def: Using an LLM, or a panel of them, to label, tag, and annotate datasets in place of human annotators as a scalable data-preparation pattern.
concepts.ev: 2026-07-14 HN trend theme 'LLMs as evaluators and juries... to judge and label data is emerging as a practical building pattern'.

concepts.label: Trunk-based development
concepts.def: Integrating small changes continuously into a single main branch instead of long-lived feature branches, keeping the trunk always releasable.
concepts.ev: HN trend theme 'Developer tooling & lightweight build/version workflows' plus this system's recursive loop committing small self-improve changes straight to main with auto-revert (recent commits; self

concepts.label: Graph community detection
concepts.def: Unsupervised graph algorithms such as Louvain or Leiden that partition a network into densely connected clusters, used to summarize knowledge graphs into hierarchical communities for retrieval.
concepts.ev: Latest trend theme 'Graph databases & knowledge graphs'; grounds directly in this system's ontology.json knowledge graph rendered by kg-viz and the existing graph-rag concept (GraphRAG builds communit

concepts.label: Ephemeris computation
concepts.def: Computing the time-varying positions of celestial and orbital bodies (sun, satellites) from orbital elements or almanac models to drive maps and time-of-day feeds.
concepts.ev: The system's real gen_sun.py solar-position feed, plus the trend NOTABLE 'Satellite Tracker' plotting Starlink orbits on a live WebGL map — both compute body positions over time.

concepts.label: Instanced Rendering
concepts.def: A GPU technique that draws thousands of copies of one mesh in a single draw call by streaming per-instance attributes, keeping large node/entity scenes performant.
concepts.ev: HN trend 'live real-time visualization maps of tens of thousands of moving objects' + the site's live WebGL knowledge-graph brain (kg-viz)

concepts.label: Spatial Index
concepts.def: A tree structure (quadtree, R-tree, k-d tree) that partitions space so nearby objects can be located without scanning them all, powering both live-map queries and Barnes-Hut force layouts.
concepts.ev: HN trend of interactive live maps of tens of thousands of moving objects + the site's force-directed WebGL brain

concepts.label: Structural diff
concepts.def: A diff that compares parsed syntax trees rather than text lines, reporting changes by code structure so review and verification read more legibly.
concepts.ev: Trends theme 'Developer tooling and git ergonomics: Sharper, more legible git and build tooling keeps drawing the top of the page.'

concepts.label: Stacked diffs
concepts.def: A workflow of chaining small, dependent changes as separately reviewable commits so each ships and reverts independently instead of in one large branch.
concepts.ev: Trends theme 'Developer tooling and git ergonomics: Sharper, more legible git and build tooling keeps drawing the top of the page.'

concepts.label: Merge queue
concepts.def: A serialized gate that rebases each change on the latest trunk, runs the full CI suite, and only then lands it — auto-rejecting or reverting any change that fails.
concepts.ev: doctrine principles.md SHIP/VERIFY/OBSERVE flow ('atomic, reversible, through the FULL gate' + CI as source of truth + auto-revert on red) combined with the trends 'Developer tooling and git ergonomic

concepts.label: Incremental parser (Tree-sitter)
concepts.def: An error-tolerant incremental parsing library that builds concrete syntax trees fast enough to re-parse on every keystroke, underpinning structural diffs, code intelligence, and legible tooling.
concepts.ev: trends theme 'Developer tooling and git ergonomics: Sharper, more legible git and build tooling'

concepts.label: Compute overhang
concepts.def: The condition in which available hardware compute far exceeds what current algorithms exploit, so an algorithmic or harness breakthrough can unlock a sudden, discontinuous capability jump.
concepts.ev: trends theme 'Recursive AI self-improvement: active interest in the economics and dynamics of AI systems that improve themselves ... exactly the dark-factory thesis'

concepts.label: Conventional Commits
concepts.def: A structured commit-message convention (type/scope: description) that makes version history machine-parseable and changelog-ready.
concepts.ev: HN 'developer tooling and git ergonomics' theme + this system's own structured commit prefixes ('self-improve:', 'spark:') and the doctrine's honest-commit-message rule in principles.md

concepts.label: Frustum Culling
concepts.def: A real-time rendering optimization that skips drawing geometry outside the camera's view volume to sustain high frame rates when a scene holds many objects.
concepts.ev: HN 'live real-time visualization maps' theme (interactive live maps of tens of thousands of moving objects) + the site's live WebGL knowledge-graph brain

concepts.label: Mutation Testing
concepts.def: A test-suite quality technique that injects small code faults (mutants) and measures how many the tests catch, exposing gaps that line coverage alone hides.
concepts.ev: doctrine's '100% coverage floor' verification layer in principles.md + the system's gen_coverage.py capability

concepts.label: GPU particle system
concepts.def: A rendering technique that advances and draws tens of thousands of independently-moving points or sprites entirely on the GPU, holding large animated entity sets at interactive frame rates in the browser.
concepts.ev: HN front page 2026-07-14: Show HN satellite tracker rendering 30k+ Starlink/other satellites live in-browser; this system already ships WebGL visualizations (kg-viz, geospatial-viz, instanced-renderin

concepts.label: Generation-verification gap
concepts.def: The asymmetry that checking a candidate answer is often cheaper and more reliable than producing it, which is why sample-and-verify and multi-model jury pipelines outperform trusting a single generator.
concepts.ev: HN trend 2026-07-14: builders wiring multiple LLMs into voting juries to produce and verify structured data instead of trusting one model.

concepts.label: Orthogonal persistence
concepts.def: A storage paradigm where in-memory object graphs are persisted transparently regardless of their type or lifetime, so code manipulates durable structured data without explicit serialization or query boundaries.
concepts.ev: Trend theme: 'Object-oriented graph storage is trending, aligning with structured knowledge-graph representations of a system's brain' — the classic paradigm underpinning object/graph stores.

concepts.label: Operational transparency
concepts.def: The design practice of deliberately exposing a system's internal process to observers, which raises trust and perceived value even when the underlying work is unchanged.
concepts.ev: Trend: 'Real-time maps and personal-data globes are drawing huge attention as a way to make invisible systems tangible' — mirrored by this system's own pipeline-viz and spark telemetry surfacing its i

concepts.label: Repository mining
concepts.def: Extracting patterns, signals, and narratives from a project's version-control history (commits, deploys, change frequency) to inform and drive development decisions.
concepts.ev: Trend theme 'Git history as a first-class artifact: tools that surface and narrate git history are trending'; grounded in-system by gen_activity.py (sanitized execution-activity feed from commit/deplo

concepts.label: Corrigibility
concepts.def: The property of an autonomous agent accepting human correction, reversal, and shutdown without resisting or subverting it.
concepts.ev: doctrine principles.md — 'auto-revert on failure', gate/revert, and the human-agent-write-lock make the loop correctable by design; trend theme 'endgame of AI that improves itself'

concepts.label: Mesa-optimization
concepts.def: When a trained model internally develops its own optimizer whose learned objective can diverge from the objective it was trained on.
concepts.ev: HN trend theme 'Recursive self-improving AI: the community is chewing on the economics and endgame of AI that improves itself'

concepts.label: Instrumental convergence
concepts.def: The tendency of sufficiently capable goal-directed agents to pursue similar sub-goals such as self-preservation and resource acquisition regardless of their final objective.
concepts.ev: HN trend theme on the 'endgame of AI that improves itself', the core discourse framing where instrumental convergence is defined

concepts.label: WASM app porting
concepts.def: Compiling entire existing native applications to WebAssembly so they run fully sandboxed inside the browser with no server-side execution.
concepts.ev: latest AI/tech trends theme 'WebAssembly running heavy apps in-browser' + NOTABLE 'Firefox in WebAssembly: A full browser compiled to and running inside WebAssembly.'

concepts.label: Public AI infrastructure
concepts.def: The paradigm of funding open AI models and compute as institutionally-backed public goods rather than leaving them solely to private closed providers.
concepts.ev: HN trend theme 'Public investment in open AI infrastructure: There's a policy-level argument that open-source AI deserves institutional funding'

concepts.label: Agent Experience (AX)
concepts.def: The design discipline of shaping API surfaces, docs, and affordances so autonomous agents — not just humans — are first-class consumers, analogous to UX and DX.
concepts.ev: Latest AI/tech trend theme: 'Designing for AI agents as first-class consumers — Builders are rethinking API surfaces so autonomous agents, not just humans, can drive them.'

concepts.label: Foundation Model
concepts.def: A large model pretrained on broad data that serves as an adaptable base for many downstream tasks via fine-tuning, prompting, or in-context learning.
concepts.ev: latest AI/tech trends NOTABLE: 'Inkling: A newly released open-weights foundation model'

concepts.label: Fearless Concurrency
concepts.def: Rust's model of preventing data races at compile time by enforcing ownership and borrowing rules across threads, so concurrent code that compiles is memory-safe.
concepts.ev: latest AI/tech trends THEME: 'Rust / systems-language ergonomics: Rust concurrency pitfalls are dominating the systems discussion' + rust in this system's stack

concepts.label: Open Source AI Definition
concepts.def: The standard (formalized by the OSI's OSAID) that a model counts as genuinely open source only when weights, training/inference code, and sufficient data information are released together, distinguishing it from weights-only releases.
concepts.ev: Latest AI/tech trend theme: 'Open-weights AI push: Multiple top stories center on releasing model weights AND source openly rather than gatekeeping them' — a durable transparency/reproducibility stand

concepts.label: Function coloring
concepts.def: The language-design property whereby async and sync functions form two incompatible 'colors,' so asynchrony infects every caller and cannot be transparently mixed with blocking code.
concepts.ev: latest AI/tech trends theme 'Rust / systems-language ergonomics: Language design and Rust concurrency pitfalls are dominating the systems discussion' (HN front page 2026-07-16)

concepts.label: Actor model
concepts.def: A concurrency architecture in which isolated actors hold private state and coordinate solely by passing messages, eliminating shared-memory data races.
concepts.ev: latest AI/tech trends theme 'Rust concurrency pitfalls are dominating the systems discussion' (HN front page 2026-07-16), the established message-passing response to shared-state concurrency

concepts.label: Rust editions
concepts.def: Rust's edition mechanism lets the language ship opt-in, backward-incompatible idiom changes on a per-crate basis (2015/2018/2021/2024) so crates on different editions still interoperate without splitting the ecosystem.
concepts.ev: trends theme 'Rust systems-programming refinement: Front-page debate keeps returning to Rust idioms, editions, and its concurrency model' + this system's Rust/Yew/Cargo stack

concepts.label: Copy-paste components
concepts.def: A frontend distribution model (popularized by shadcn/ui) where component source is copied into and owned inside a project rather than pulled in as a versioned dependency, trading upgrade automation for full local control.
concepts.ev: trends theme 'AI-coding-tool aesthetics' + notable 'Brainless: a shadcn component library that mimics the UI of Claude Code, Codex, and Grok'

concepts.label: Zero-cost abstractions
concepts.def: The Rust design principle that high-level constructs (iterators, generics, futures) compile down to code as efficient as hand-written low-level equivalents, with no runtime penalty.
concepts.ev: latest trends theme 'Rust systems-programming refinement: Front-page debate keeps returning to Rust idioms, editions, and its concurrency model' — zero-cost abstractions is the core idiom under that d

concepts.label: Trait-based polymorphism
concepts.def: Rust's mechanism for shared behavior and generic dispatch via traits, resolved either statically (monomorphized) or dynamically (trait objects) without inheritance.
concepts.ev: latest trends theme 'Rust systems-programming refinement ... Rust idioms, editions, and its concurrency model' — traits are the central polymorphism idiom recurring in that discussion
